title: Segfault when combining `--trace` and `-fno-inline`
body:
Hello! As always, thank you for Verilator!!

On git `master` and 5.018 on macOS/AArch64, I'm running into a segfault when I try to use both `--trace` and `-fno-inline`. The actual Verilog doesn't seem to matter; this input file suffices:

```verilog
module  TOP;
endmodule
```

Both of these runs work fine:

```
$ verilator_bin min.sv --debug --gdbbt --cc --trace
$ verilator_bin min.sv --debug --gdbbt --cc -fno-inline
```

But combining both `--trace` and `-fno-inline` results in this error:

```
$ verilator_bin min.sv --debug --gdbbt --cc --trace -fno-inline
Starting Verilator 5.021 devel rev v5.020-18-g68e4eeb53
- Verilator.cpp:616:  Option --verilate: Start Verilation
- V3File.cpp:234:        --check-times failed: different command line
[...]
- V3Ast.cpp:1280:     Dumping obj_dir/Vmin_051_splitas.tree
- V3TraceDecl.cpp:692:traceDeclAll: 
zsh: segmentation fault  [path]/bin/verilator_bin min.sv
```

Here is some location information from lldb:

```
Process 64134 stopped
* thread #1, queue = 'com.apple.main-thread', stop reason = EXC_BAD_ACCESS (code=1, address=0xf0)
    frame #0: 0x00000001003d3118 verilator_bin`TraceDeclVisitor::fixupPlaceholder(std::__1::basic_string<char, std::__1::char_traits<char>, std::__1::allocator<char> > const&, AstNodeStmt*) + 120
verilator_bin`TraceDeclVisitor::fixupPlaceholder:
->  0x1003d3118 <+120>: ldr    x8, [x8, #0xf0]
    0x1003d311c <+124>: mov    w19, #0x2
    0x1003d3120 <+128>: cbz    x8, 0x1003d3130           ; <+144>
    0x1003d3124 <+132>: ldrh   w8, [x8, #0x40]
Target 0: (verilator_bin) stopped.
(lldb) bt
* thread #1, queue = 'com.apple.main-thread', stop reason = EXC_BAD_ACCESS (code=1, address=0xf0)
  * frame #0: 0x00000001003d3118 verilator_bin`TraceDeclVisitor::fixupPlaceholder(std::__1::basic_string<char, std::__1::char_traits<char>, std::__1::allocator<char> > const&, AstNodeStmt*) + 120
    frame #1: 0x00000001003d0480 verilator_bin`TraceDeclVisitor::fixupPlaceholders() + 256
    frame #2: 0x00000001003cfd28 verilator_bin`TraceDeclVisitor::TraceDeclVisitor(AstNetlist*) + 236
    frame #3: 0x00000001003cf918 verilator_bin`V3TraceDecl::traceDeclAll(AstNetlist*) + 52
    frame #4: 0x00000001000ef23c verilator_bin`verilate(std::__1::basic_string<char, std::__1::char_traits<char>, std::__1::allocator<char> > const&) + 2768
    frame #5: 0x00000001000ee044 verilator_bin`main + 444
    frame #6: 0x00000001839ebf28 dyld`start + 2236
(lldb) 
```

> What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
> What OS and distribution are you using?

I reproduced this on revision 68e4eeb53e61ad3cbc7b00bb1ca70004e64c042b, the latest as of yesterday, and on a released version (5.018). All of this is on macOS 13.4.1 on an ARM machine.

Notably, I also have access to another machine (x86_64 Linux) with Verilator 5.006, where this problem does not exist.

> May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Definitely! I admit I'm not sure exactly where to start, other than that this is clearly happening somewhere in the tracing machinery.
